atmosphere: The air is thick with the smell of mildew and old stone.
A faint dripping echoes from somewhere deeper within.
Dust motes hang suspended in the still air.
The silence here feels deliberate, as if something is listening.
details: Rusted iron sconces line the walls, their torches long since burned out.
Faded murals depict a civilization that clearly met a bad end.
The floor is cracked, with pale roots pushing through the gaps.
Something has scratched deep grooves into the stone near the entrance.
exits: A low archway to the north is sealed with a rusted portcullis.
To the east, a wooden door hangs half off its hinges.
A spiral staircase descends into darkness in the far corner.

atmosphere: The smell of roasting meat and spilled ale hangs in the air.
A bard in the corner is playing something almost recognizable.
Laughter and argument compete for dominance across the room.
Firelight throws long shadows across the low-beamed ceiling.
details: A group of off-duty guards argue loudly over a game of dice.
The barkeep polishes a tankard with a rag that makes it dirtier.
Someone has carved their name — and a rude drawing — into your table.
A hooded figure in the corner hasn't touched their drink.
exits: Stairs at the back lead up to the rooms for let.
A side door near the kitchen is propped open with a cask.
The main entrance lets in a gust of cold air every time it swings open.

atmosphere: The canopy blocks most of the light, leaving the world grey and damp.
Birds have gone silent here. Only the wind moves.
Twisted roots make the ground treacherous underfoot.
Something about this place makes the hairs on the back of your neck rise.
details: The trees here are old — far older than the kingdom above ground.
Crude markers made of sticks and bone line the path at intervals.
A circle of mushrooms rings a patch of ground where nothing else grows.
You notice that no two trees cast shadows in the same direction.
exits: A deer trail winds northeast, barely visible in the undergrowth.
The sound of running water comes from somewhere to the west.
The path behind you seems subtly different from how you remember it.

atmosphere: Vendors shout over one another in a half-dozen languages.
The smell of spices, fish, and livestock compete for dominance.
Children dart between adult legs, chasing each other or picking pockets.
Every surface is draped with goods — cloth, produce, caged animals.
details: A street performer swallows fire to a modest crowd nearby.
Two merchants are having a furious, whispered argument over a ledger.
A city guard leans against a post, watching everyone with tired eyes.
Pinned to a noticeboard: wanted posters, a missing child notice, a tax decree.
exits: The main road leads north toward the city gate.
A narrow alley between two stalls disappears into shadow.
Wide steps lead up to the civic hall at the square's eastern edge.

atmosphere: Emergency lighting casts everything in dim red.
The life support hum is gone. The silence is louder than it should be.
Condensation drips from ruptured coolant lines in the ceiling.
Every surface is filmed with a grey dust that doesn't quite look like dust.
details: Scorch marks radiate outward from a sealed bulkhead door.
Personal effects — a photo, a meal tin, a boot — lie where they were dropped.
A terminal on the wall cycles through an error message no one has cleared.
The floor grating has been torn up and never replaced.
exits: An airlock to starboard shows a green light. It shouldn't.
A maintenance shaft runs aft, just wide enough to crawl through.
The blast doors ahead are manually wedged open with a cargo strut.

atmosphere: Advertising drones drift overhead, reading biometrics before choosing their pitch.
The crowd moves in the practised way of people who have learned not to make eye contact.
A synthetic voice announces something in three languages. No one looks up.
The air recyclers are losing the fight against the smell of food and bodies.
details: Security drones trace slow arcs above the crowd, lenses sweeping.
A protest — or what's left of one — has been painted over but not quite erased.
A woman in a corporate uniform stares at her comm implant, expression unreadable.
Someone has jammed the nearest surveillance node with a loop of static.
exits: A mag-lev platform to the upper levels requires a tier-two access chip.
A service corridor behind the vendor stalls is technically off-limits.
The main exit feeds into an ID checkpoint queue thirty people deep.

atmosphere: The blinds cut the streetlight into thin stripes across the floor.
Somewhere a radiator is knocking. It hasn't been fixed in years.
Rain taps against the window in the irregular way that keeps you awake.
The whole place smells of cigarettes, cheap cologne, and bad decisions.
details: A glass sits on the desk with just enough left in it to be tempting.
The phone hasn't rung in three days. Tonight it does.
Someone has been through the files. They were careful. Not careful enough.
A hat and coat on the rack belong to someone who hasn't come back for them.
exits: The fire escape rattles in the wind outside the window.
A door to the back opens onto an alley that smells like the city gave up.
The main stairs creak on the third step — useful for knowing who's coming.

atmosphere: The streetlights are doing their best. It isn't enough.
A car idles at the far end of the block. It's been there a while.
Fog off the water turns every light into a smear.
Footsteps behind you. Then none. Then again.
details: A newspaper blows past. The headline is about someone you've heard of.
Chalk markings on the pavement look too deliberate to be children's drawings.
A payphone rings as you pass. You keep walking. It keeps ringing.
Someone dropped a matchbook here recently. The name on it rings a bell.
exits: A cab idles at the curb. The driver doesn't look at you.
An unlit stairwell leads down to a door with no number.
The bridge ahead has one working lamp and two men standing under it.

atmosphere: The temperature drops sharply as you cross the threshold.
Something in the room has moved since the last person was here. Nothing living.
A low tone — not quite a sound — sits at the edge of perception.
The shadows in the corners are darker than the light allows.
details: Every mirror in the room has been covered with a cloth.
A child's drawing on the wall depicts the room as it looks now, dated thirty years ago.
The dust on the floor is undisturbed — except for a single set of footprints going nowhere.
Flowers in a vase are perfectly fresh. The water is black.
exits: The door you came through is open. You're certain you closed it.
A second door leads somewhere the floorplan doesn't account for.
The window looks out onto a view that doesn't match the street outside.

atmosphere: The light here is wrong — too flat, from no direction in particular.
Sound travels strangely. Voices carry from far away. Nearby things are silent.
You have the distinct sense this place exists between other places.
Time feels unreliable. You may have been standing here longer than you think.
details: Offerings have been left here — old coins, tied cloth, a tooth.
The ground is worn smooth by feet that aren't yours.
Something is visible at the edge of vision that isn't there when looked at directly.
A name has been spoken here many times. You don't know whose. You nearly do.
exits: One direction feels like forward. The others feel like choices.
A path leads somewhere. Whether it leads there the same way twice is unclear.
Turning back feels possible, technically.
